title: Automate AI Workflows with n8n

modules.title: n8n's Execution Model: Nodes, Triggers, and Data Flow Fundamentals
modules.sub: Understand how n8n moves data through connected nodes
modules.description: Learners explore n8n's core architecture by tracing how triggers fire workflows and how data passes between nodes. They build and run simple workflows to observe execution order, item handling, and output structure. By the end, they can read and reason about any n8n workflow canvas with confidence.

modules.title: Integrating LLMs: Connecting OpenAI, Anthropic, and Local Models to Workflows
modules.sub: Wire AI models into workflows using credentials and API calls
modules.description: Learners connect live LLM providers—including OpenAI, Anthropic, and a locally hosted model—to n8n nodes using secure credential management. They configure prompts, model parameters, and response parsing so that AI output becomes usable structured data downstream. They leave with a reusable LLM integration pattern they can adapt to any model.

modules.title: Building Intelligent Pipelines: Conditional Logic, Branching, and Error Handling
modules.sub: Make workflows that decide, branch, and recover from failures
modules.description: Learners add IF nodes, Switch nodes, and merge paths to build workflows that behave differently based on AI output or incoming data. They deliberately trigger error conditions and implement fallback branches and retry logic to make pipelines resilient. The result is a workflow that handles real-world unpredictability without silent failures.

modules.title: Working with External Data: APIs, Databases, Webhooks, and File Ingestion
modules.sub: Pull and push data between n8n and the systems you already use
modules.description: Learners connect n8n to external REST APIs, a relational database, an inbound webhook, and uploaded files to practice each major data ingestion pattern. They map, transform, and normalize incoming data so it is ready for downstream AI processing. This module equips them to plug any organizational data source into a workflow.

modules.title: Automating Real Work: Email Triage, Report Generation, and Document Processing
modules.sub: Build end-to-end automations that handle common professional tasks
modules.description: Learners construct three practical, full-length workflows: an LLM-powered email triage system, an automated report generator that pulls live data, and a document processing pipeline that extracts and categorizes content. Each workflow is evaluated against real business criteria such as accuracy, latency, and output format. Learners finish with deployable templates they can adapt immediately.

modules.title: Human-in-the-Loop Design: When and How to Insert Approval Steps
modules.sub: Design workflows that pause for human review before acting
modules.description: Learners identify which decisions in an automated workflow carry enough risk or ambiguity to warrant human review, then implement wait-for-approval steps using webhooks and notification nodes. They design the UX of approval requests—what information reviewers need and how responses route the workflow forward. The module produces a reusable human-in-the-loop pattern suitable for compliance-sensitive processes.

modules.title: Apply It: Map and Automate a Repetitive Workflow from Your Own Role
modules.sub: Identify a real task and build your own working automation
modules.description: Learners select a recurring task from their own job, map its inputs, decision points, and outputs, and then build a functioning n8n workflow that automates it end-to-end. They apply skills from all previous modules—LLM integration, branching logic, external data, and human-in-the-loop steps—as their task requires. The deliverable is a personally relevant, production-ready pipeline they present with a brief rationale for their design choices.

modules.title: Self-Hosting, Security Considerations, and Scaling n8n in an Organization
modules.sub: Deploy, secure, and scale n8n for organizational use beyond a single user
modules.description: Learners walk through the key decisions involved in self-hosting n8n—infrastructure options, environment variables, and database configuration—versus using n8n Cloud, evaluating each against organizational requirements. They apply security best practices including credential encryption, role-based access control, and network exposure hardening. Finally, they consider how to structure workflows, environments, and governance policies to support a team of multiple workflow builders.

synopsis: n8n gives professionals a visual, code-optional environment to wire AI models into real business workflows â without surrendering control to opaque SaaS products. This course teaches you to design robust, production-grade automation pipelines that combine LLMs with the systems your organization already uses.
rationale: Workflow automation is the highest-leverage AI application for most working professionals, yet the tooling â particularly open-source options like n8n â remains poorly understood outside developer circles, leaving significant productivity gains on the table.
learning_outcome: Professionals can design and deploy a working n8n automation pipeline that integrates one or more AI models into a recurring task or business process within their own organization.
nearest_existing: The Four Signals of AI-Native Opportunity
